title: cephalomanes boninense
intro: Cephalomanes boninense, also known as the Bonin cliff fern, is a species of fern native to the Bonin Islands in Japan. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Cephalomanes boninense include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and alleviate congestion, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Cephalomanes boninense is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal benefits, and it can also be dried and used in capsules or powder form.
